title: /search: add has_summary_stats filter + expose data_type on phenotype results
description: Let /search support the phenotype-search use case. (1) Add data_type to phenotype search-index entries and emit it in JSON + TSV output (frontend needs it to call summary_stats/{resource}/{data_type}). (2) Add query param has_summary_stats (bool, default false); build module-level _SUMSTATS_PAIRS = set(summary_stats.get_available_resources_and_types()); annotate every phenotype result with has_summary_stats = (resource,data_type) in _SUMSTATS_PAIRS; when the param is true, drop phenotype results where false. (3) Update TSV header/rows + OpenAPI schema/example. Files: app/routers/search.py + phenotype search-index build. Context: consumed by genetics-results-browser refactor (epic genetics-results-browser-3uu, frontend issue 3uu.24); full spec in that repo's refactor.backend.md §2.

title: gnomAD allele frequencies as a variant_annotation source (multi-variant)
description: Serve gnomAD per-population AF via the existing POST /variant_annotation/{source} (already multi-variant). Steps: (1) make cpra column indices per-source — move _CHR_COL.._ALT_COL off VariantAnnotationService into the source config (default [1,2,3,4] so finngen is unchanged); (2) register source 'gnomad' with cpra_cols [0,1,2,3] pointing at gs://finngen-commons/gnomad/gnomad.genomes.exomes.v4.0.sites.v2.tsv.bgz (header: #chr,pos,ref,alt,rsids,filters,AN,AF,AF_afr,AF_amr,AF_asj,AF_eas,AF_fin,AF_mid,AF_nfe,AF_remaining,AF_sas,most_severe,gene_most_severe,consequences,genome_or_exome); (3) DATA: create tabix index 'tabix -s1 -b2 -e2' on the .bgz and upload the .tbi (none exists yet). Note: file merges genomes+exomes (genome_or_exome g/e) so a variant may return two rows — consumer merges. Files: app/services/variant_annotation_service.py, app/config/profiles/*/common.py. Context: consumed by the genetics-results-browser refactor (epic genetics-results-browser-3uu, frontend issue 3uu.11); full spec in that repo's refactor.backend.md §1.
notes: tabix .tbi index already exists in GCS (gs://finngen-commons/gnomad/gnomad.genomes.exomes.v4.0.sites.v2.tsv.bgz.tbi, uploaded 2026-05-31) — step (3) is already done; this is now a code-only task.
close_reason: Done: gnomad added as variant_annotation source; live multi-variant POST verified returning per-population AF.

title: Add filter to query only coding variants
description: Surfaced from genetics-mcp-server conversation analysis (session 44bf749b-e474-42b8-8244-3166041363e3). The chat agent needs to restrict queries to coding variants but there is no filter for it, so it over-fetches and truncates. Add an API filter to return only coding variants. The list of coding variant consequence categories should be defined in a shared place (it exists somewhere already — locate and reuse). Depends on variant annotations being available in BigQuery (see genetics-results-db issue 'Add variant annotations into BigQuery').
notes: Skipped (ops/data dependency unmet). Blocked by genetics-results-db-e99 'Add variant annotations into BigQuery' which is still OPEN. The coding-consequence category list already exists and is reusable: coding_set in app/config/common.py. This tabix-based API has no coding filter wired to any endpoint yet. Also which endpoint should receive the filter is unspecified: credible_sets/summary_stats already carry an inline most_severe column (a filter there would need no BigQuery), whereas the issue explicitly ties the feature to BigQuery variant annotations, so the intended data path is ambiguous. Do not implement until genetics-results-db-e99 lands and the target endpoint is confirmed.
close_reason: Implemented coding_only query param on credible_sets variant-row endpoints (by_phenotype, by_id, by_region, by_variant GET/POST, by_gene, by_qtl_gene), filtering on the inline most_severe column against the shared config.common.coding_set. Header-aware filter_stream_by_coding (TSV) + filter_coding_rows (JSON) in app/core/streams.py, wired through range_response. Default behavior unchanged; NA/missing most_severe excluded. Unit tests added (tests/test_coding_filter.py, 8 passing). Committed 930f867. summary_stats intentionally out of scope: most_severe is present only in some sumstats files and those endpoints are queried by explicit variant list, not the over-fetch case.

title: Support dataset display-name overrides (rename datasets for the frontend)
description:
The credible-set 'dataset' field is a raw column value baked into the source data files (e.g. 'UKB_PPP', 'FinnGen_Olink'), mapped to (resource, version) via dataset_to_resource in app/config/profiles/*/common.py. The frontend displays this raw string verbatim (spaces for underscores). FinnGen proteomics datasets carry their platform inline ('FinnGen_Olink' -> 'FinnGen Olink'), but UKB-PPP is just 'UKB_PPP' -> 'UKB PPP', so the Olink 3K (Explore 3072) panel is not visible. This matters because it explains why some proteins have a FinnGen pQTL but no UKBB one (not covered by the 3K panel vs the 5K panel).

The API should expose a configurable per-dataset display name / label (e.g. in datasets.yaml or dataset_to_resource) so datasets can be renamed centrally instead of each frontend hard-coding overrides. As an interim fix, the browser frontend hard-codes 'UKB_PPP' -> 'UKBB PPP (Olink 3K)' in VariantCredibleSetTable.tsx (DATASET_LABEL_OVERRIDES); that override should be removed once the API supplies display names.
close_reason: Implemented configurable per-dataset display-name overrides. Added dataset_display_names config map (keyed by raw dataset column value, same key space as dataset_to_resource) in both profiles + app/config/common.py, seeded with UKB_PPP -> 'UKBB PPP (Olink 3K)'. Surfaced via new GET /api/v1/dataset_display_names endpoint. Frontend can now drop its hard-coded DATASET_LABEL_OVERRIDES (separate frontend repo work). Committed ab37a18.

title: R12 FinnGen pheno mapping file has blank phenostring for CURRENT_SMOKER / EVER_SMOKER
description:
Source data issue (distinct from the code fix in 99da5f4).

The file gs://finngen-commons/results_api_data/mapping_files/finngen_r12_pheno.json lists CURRENT_SMOKER and EVER_SMOKER with an empty "phenostring" (R14's finngen_r14_pheno.json has 'Current smoker' / 'Ever smoker' correctly). Because both files feed the finngen resource and R12 is merged after R14 in /trait_name_mapping, the blank used to overwrite R14's good name (2 empty values out of ~29k).

Code-side mitigation already shipped (99da5f4): the handler now keeps the first non-empty phenostring per code and never stores an empty one, so the API output is correct regardless. This issue tracks fixing the underlying DATA so the R12 file no longer carries blank phenostrings (and to check whether other codes in that file are similarly blank).

Owner: whoever maintains the finngen-commons mapping files.

Acceptance: finngen_r12_pheno.json has non-empty phenostring for CURRENT_SMOKER, EVER_SMOKER (and any other blank rows); no regression in /trait_name_mapping.
close_reason: Closed per owner: code-side mitigation already shipped (99da5f4) so API output is correct regardless; fixing blank phenostrings in the upstream GCS file finngen_r12_pheno.json is owned by the finngen-commons maintainers, tracked externally if needed.

title: Update docs/project-spec.md for the new pQTL meta dataset
description: Update /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-api/docs/project-spec.md to mention the finngen_ukbb_pqtl 3-way pQTL meta summary-stats dataset under resource finngen_ukbb (summary-stats only, unfiltered/union tier). Keep consistent with the existing summary-stats/datasets descriptions.

title: Integration-test live summary-stats query for a pan-asset pQTL protein
description: Start the server (python run_server.py) and query the summary-stats endpoint for a known protein (e.g. PCSK9) in resource finngen_ukbb / dataset finngen_ukbb_pqtl over a small region, verifying rows return with the mapped meta + per-study columns. Add/extend an integration test under tests/ following existing summary-stats test patterns. Run ruff check.

title: Verify products correctness, no credible-set wiring, and mcp-server needs no change
description: After config is in place, confirm: (1) app/services/config_util.py dataset_products(finngen_ukbb_pqtl) returns summary_stats only and NO credible_sets/colocalization; (2) the component datasets finngen_pqtl/finngen_pqtl_5k/ukbb_pqtl keep their existing credible sets untouched; (3) confirm no change is needed in /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-mcp-server (it discovers datasets dynamically via list_datasets/products; see src/genetics_mcp_server/config/defaults.py). Document the conclusion on the epic.
notes: VERIFIED 5/5 PASS. dataset_products('finngen_ukbb_pqtl') = {'summary_stats': True} under both finngen+daly profiles (no credible_sets/colocalization). Referenced only in summary_stats.py (both profiles); absent from credible_sets.py/coloc.py. Component datasets unchanged: finngen_pqtl/finngen_pqtl_5k/ukbb_pqtl all retain credible_sets:True. mcp-server needs NO change: list_datasets is a thin HTTP passthrough to /v1/datasets; products discovered dynamically; no hardcoded dataset ids in src/. Note: defaults.py flags finngen_ukbb* as pseudo-CS but since products shows no credible sets for this dataset, no false claim arises.

title: Add identical finngen_ukbb_pqtl summary_stats entry to daly profile
description: Mirror the finngen-profile data_files entry into /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-api/app/config/profiles/daly/summary_stats.py (same id/dataset_id/resource/prefix/suffix/column_mapping) so both deployment profiles serve the dataset, matching how finngen_pqtl_sumstats etc. are mirrored across profiles.

title: Sync datasets.yaml to api repo via sync-datasets.sh
description: Run /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-suite/scripts/sync-datasets.sh to propagate the updated registry to /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-api/configs/datasets.yaml (and any other service repos the script targets). Do NOT hand-edit the api repo copy. Verify the finngen_ukbb_pqtl entry appears in the synced api file under both profiles.

title: Add finngen_ukbb_pqtl summary_stats data_files entry (finngen profile) with column_mapping
description:
In /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-api/app/config/profiles/finngen/summary_stats.py add a data_files entry:
  id: finngen_ukbb_pqtl_sumstats, dataset_id: finngen_ukbb_pqtl, resource: finngen_ukbb, data_source: gcloud,
  prefix: gs://finngen-production-library-green/omics/proteomics/pan_asset_pQTL_meta_ukbb-ppp_FG-batch1-5/sumstats/unfiltered/
  suffix: _meta_out.tsv.gz
  Use the finngen_mvp_ukbb_gwas_sumstats entry (lines 27-87) as the template for column_mapping naming conventions.
  Map: chr/pos/ref/alt, SNP->rsid; per-study FG2_3K_* (beta/sebeta->se/pval/ALT_FREQ->af), UKB_PPP_* (same), FG3_CB_* (NOTE: FG3_CB_af_alt not ALT_FREQ); all_meta_N->meta_n, all_inv_var_meta_beta->beta, _sebeta->se, _p->pval, _mlogp->mlog10p, all_inv_var_het_p->het_p; leave_{FG2_3K,UKB_PPP,FG3_CB}_inv_var_meta_{beta,sebeta->se,p->pval,mlogp->mlog10p,het_p}. Leave the trailing duplicate rsid column and leave_*_N columns unmapped (dropped).
  CRITICAL: verify whether the sumstats parser sees the first header column as '#CHR' or 'CHR' (the file header literally starts with '#CHR'; the sibling finngen meta maps 'CHR'). Check how the header is read (app/services/sumstats_data_access.py) and key the mapping on whatever the parser actually sees. Do NOT set 'version' (derived from registry by app/config/summary_stats.py loader).

title: Register finngen_ukbb_pqtl dataset in suite datasets.yaml (both profiles) + broaden finngen_ukbb resource desc
description:
In /home/jkarjala/suite/genetics-results-suite/configs/datasets.yaml (single source of truth):
1. Broaden the finngen_ukbb entry in the resources: block (~line 14) description from 'FinnGen + UK Biobank GWAS meta-analysis' to include pQTL (e.g. 'FinnGen + UK Biobank GWAS and pQTL meta-analysis').
2. Add a new dataset entry finngen_ukbb_pqtl under the finngen profile QTL section (~line 744, near finngen_pqtl/finngen_pqtl_5k/ukbb_pqtl) with: resource: finngen_ukbb, version (e.g. 'v1' or a date tag), data_type: pqtl, trait_type: quantitative, n_samples: 48703, author, publication_date '2026-06', metadata_file: null, metadata_harmonizer: null, and a description stating it is a 3-way inverse-variance pQTL meta-analysis of FG2-3K (Olink 3K) + UKB-PPP (Olink 3K) + FG3 Cross-Batch (Olink HT 5K), 2,831 proteins common to all platforms, summary statistics only (no fine-mapping/credible sets; plots/QC forthcoming).
3. Add the IDENTICAL finngen_ukbb_pqtl dataset entry under the daly profile QTL section (~line 1511) for parity.
Match the exact YAML schema/indentation of the sibling finngen_pqtl_5k entry.

title: Feature: Serve pan-asset 3-way pQTL meta-analysis (FG2-3K + UKB-PPP + FG3 Cross-Batch)
description:
Serve the new genome-wide pQTL meta-analysis summary statistics at gs://finngen-production-library-green/omics/proteomics/pan_asset_pQTL_meta_ukbb-ppp_FG-batch1-5/sumstats/unfiltered/ via the genetics-results-api summary-stats endpoint, and register the dataset in the single-source-of-truth configs/datasets.yaml (suite repo) synced to the api repo.

Data: 2,831 per-protein bgzip+tabix TSV files (_meta_out.tsv.gz), 42-column 3-way inverse-variance meta (FG2-3K, UKB-PPP, FG3 Cross-Batch) with per-study beta/sebeta/pval/AF, all_inv_var_meta_*, het_p, and leave-one-out columns. Format matches the existing finngen_mvp_ukbb multi-study meta pattern. Use the unfiltered (union) tier per README section 8 (filtered is a lead-study subset, not a union).

Context/constraints:
- The FinnGen component datasets (finngen_pqtl 3K, finngen_pqtl_5k) already have credible sets; UKB-PPP (ukbb_pqtl) has credible sets too. This NEW meta is summary-stats-only (plots/qc/credible sets forthcoming). Datasets must be kept in sync re: products — the meta should expose only summary_stats, not falsely claim credible sets.
- mcp-server LLM instructions discover datasets dynamically via list_datasets/products, so likely need NO changes — verify.
- API is read-only; configuration-driven (app/config/profiles/finngen/summary_stats.py + datasets.yaml registry).

title: Docs: HGNC group/annotation endpoints
description: Document the HGNC gene-group data loading + /gene_group/members and /gene/normalize endpoints in genetics-results-api docs (project-spec/README).

title: Tests for gene_group/members + gene/normalize endpoints
description: Add API tests (mirror existing router tests) for both endpoints: group_id and group_name resolution incl. a hierarchical case (group 139 returns HTR1A via lineage though its leaf is 170), and alias/previous-symbol normalization.

title: Endpoint: GET /api/v1/gene/normalize
description: Add GET /api/v1/gene/normalize?symbols=<comma-separated> resolving previous/alias symbols to current approved HGNC symbol using the in-memory HGNC data (search_service already indexes aliases->primary symbol; reuse that). Return mappings [{input, approved, matched_on}] and unresolved[]. Whole-universe coverage.

title: Endpoint: GET /api/v1/gene_group/members
description: Add a router (app/routers/gene_groups.py, or extend genes.py) exposing GET /api/v1/gene_group/members?group_id=<int> | group_name=<str>. Resolve to member genes via the in-memory lineage map (any group id returns all genes whose lineage contains it). Return each gene with symbol, ensembl_id, chr, gene_start, gene_end (coords already in memory). Add to app/server.py include_router list. Keep olfactory receptors; note in docstring.

title: Load HGNC gene-group + lineage data into the API
description:
Add HGNC gene-group + lineage loading to the API, MIRRORING the existing hgnc_file GCS/fsspec mechanism. Confirmed facts from recon:
- Mapping files are loaded from GCS via fsspec at startup. hgnc_file lives at gs://finngen-commons/results_api_data/mapping_files/hgnc_complete_set.txt (finngen profile, app/config/profiles/finngen/genes.py:9) and gs://daly-genetics-results/mapping_files/hgnc_complete_set.txt (daly profile, app/config/profiles/daly/genes.py:9). Default GENCODE version = 49 (app/services/gene_name_and_position_mapping.py get_coordinates_lookup default = genes['gencode_versions'][0]).
DECISION (user, mirror-to-GCS): add three config keys per profile pointing at, under the SAME mapping_files/ dir:
  hgnc_gene_has_family.tsv, hgnc_hierarchy_closure.tsv, hgnc_family.tsv
  finngen: gs://finngen-commons/results_api_data/mapping_files/<name>
  daly:    gs://daly-genetics-results/mapping_files/<name>
The user will UPLOAD these files (from genenames.org/download/gene-groups/) to both buckets; do NOT assume they exist at runtime yet. Write code + UNIT tests with small in-repo fixtures (do not require live GCS).
Implementation: add config keys in app/config/profiles/{finngen,daly}/genes.py (+ app/config/common.py if it surfaces genes config), load the 3 files via fsspec like hgnc_file, and build an in-memory map hgnc_id -> FULL lineage set of (group_id, group_name): take each gene's leaf family_id from gene_has_family, expand via hierarchy_closure (child_fam_id -> all parent_fam_ids; already transitive) to all ancestors, union, map ids->names via family table. Source columns: gene_has_family(hgnc_id, family_id); hierarchy_closure(parent_fam_id, child_fam_id, distance); family(id/family_id, name) -- verify exact headers from the HGNC files. Reuse per-gene coords/aliases already in _load_genes. Wire via app/dependencies.py container.

UPDATE: the 3 HGNC gene-group files are HGNC-native CSV (comma, quoted) and named hgnc_gene_has_family.csv, hgnc_hierarchy_closure.csv, hgnc_family.csv (the DB loader nll.3 settled this). Read them as CSV here too; hgnc_complete_set.txt stays TSV. Columns: gene_has_family(hgnc_id,family_id); hierarchy_closure(parent_fam_id,child_fam_id,distance); family(id,name).

title: Feature: HGNC gene-group + annotation endpoints
description: Add HGNC gene-group + annotation data and REST endpoints so the mcp-server tools (get_gene_group_members, normalize_gene_symbols) call the API like every other specialized tool instead of BigQuery. The API already loads the HGNC complete-set + GENCODE coords in app/services/search_service.py (_load_genes) via DI (app/dependencies.py; config genes.hgnc_file + genes.gene_name_mapping_file). Extend it to also load HGNC gene-group DB files (gene_has_family hgnc_id->family_id + hierarchy_closure parent/child/distance + family names, genenames.org/download/gene-groups/) and compute each gene's FULL lineage group set (leaf + all ancestors via the precomputed closure) in memory, so any group resolves to all descendant members. BigQuery stays a separate surface (gene_annotations table in genetics-results-db, query_bigquery only, for cis/trans coloc JOINs) fed from the same HGNC source -- no specialized tool touches BigQuery.

title: Update integration tests for gene coordinates in search, nearest_genes, peak_to_genes
description: Update integration tests to verify that gene coordinate fields (chrom, gene_start, gene_end) are present in responses. For /search: assert gene results include chrom, gene_start, gene_end fields. For /nearest_genes: assert chrom is now present in response. For /peak_to_genes: assert gene_chrom, gene_start, gene_end columns appear in TSV header and JSON results. Files: tests/ (relevant test files for each endpoint)

title: Enrich peak_to_genes streaming results with gene coordinates
description: Modify the peak_to_genes streaming pipeline to enrich results with gene coordinates. Inject GeneNameAndPositionMapping into the peak_to_genes router via Depends(get_gene_name_mapping). Add gencode_version query parameter. Build a coordinates lookup dict from the mapping and use it to append gene_chrom, gene_start, gene_end columns to each streamed row. This requires modifying tsv_line_iterator_chromatin_peaks in app/core/streams.py to accept and apply the lookup dict. Update chromatin_peaks_header_schema in app/config/chromatin_peaks.py. Files: app/routers/chromatin_peaks.py, app/core/streams.py, app/config/chromatin_peaks.py

title: Add chrom to nearest_genes response and update schema
description: The get_nearest_genes method already computes on chromosome-filtered data but omits chrom from the .select() output (line 283). Add chrom to the select list so it appears in the response. Update the response schema in app/routers/genes.py to document the chrom field. Files: app/services/gene_name_and_position_mapping.py (get_nearest_genes .select()), app/routers/genes.py (response schema)

title: Add gencode_version query param to /api/v1/search and update response format
description: Add gencode_version optional query parameter to the /api/v1/search endpoint (default: None, which maps to v49 internally). Pass it through to search_index.search(). Update the response schema to include chrom, gene_start, gene_end for gene results. Update TSV format generation to include coordinate columns for genes. Files: app/routers/search.py

title: Extend SearchIndex to include gene coordinates from GeneNameAndPositionMapping
description: Change SearchIndex.__init__ to also accept a GeneNameAndPositionMapping instance. In _load_genes, after building each gene dict, look up coordinates via the mapping's get_coordinates_lookup method using ensembl_id and store chrom, gene_start, gene_end in the gene dict. Add gencode_version parameter to search() method. Update service_container.py factory to pass gene_name_mapping to SearchIndex. Files: app/services/search_service.py, app/core/service_container.py

title: Add get_coordinates_lookup method to GeneNameAndPositionMapping
description: Add a method get_coordinates_lookup(gencode_version) -> dict[str, tuple[int, int, int]] to GeneNameAndPositionMapping that builds a mapping from ENSG ID to (chrom, gene_start, gene_end) from the existing gene_positions DataFrame. This will be used by SearchIndex and peak_to_genes for coordinate enrichment. Also add chrom to the .select() list in get_nearest_genes (line 283). Files: app/services/gene_name_and_position_mapping.py

title: Feature: Add gene coordinates to search, nearest_genes, and peak_to_genes endpoints
description: Add optional gene coordinates (chrom, gene_start, gene_end) to gene results returned by /api/v1/search, /api/v1/nearest_genes, and /api/v1/peak_to_genes endpoints. An optional query parameter 'gencode_version' specifies which GENCODE version to use for coordinates (default: v49). This enriches gene results with positional information without requiring separate API calls.

title: Update project-spec and verify all query modes
description: Add variant-annotation to endpoints table in docs/project-spec.md. Run ruff check, start server, test all query modes (variant, region, gene, POST batch), test JSON format, test error cases (invalid source, missing params).

title: Remove old variant_annotation endpoints from genes.py
description: Remove old hidden variant_annotation_range and variant_annotation POST endpoints from app/routers/genes.py. These use sync subprocess against gnomAD, replaced by new endpoint.

title: Wire service container, dependencies, and server
description: Register VariantAnnotationService in app/core/service_container.py. Add get_variant_annotation_service() to app/dependencies.py. Import and include variant_annotation router in app/server.py with tags=['variant-annotation'].

title: Create variant_annotation router
description: New app/routers/variant_annotation.py. GET /variant_annotation/{source} with query params variant/region/gene/format. POST /variant_annotation/{source} with body {variants: list[str]}. Gene queries resolved via GeneNameAndPositionMapping. Use range_response() for TSV/JSON output.

title: Create VariantAnnotationService
description: New app/services/variant_annotation_service.py inheriting GCloudTabixBase. Methods: get_available_sources(), get_header(source), stream_by_range(source, chr, start, end), stream_by_variants(source, variants). Variant filtering via private _filter_by_variants() async generator. Reuse _stream_range(), _cache_header() from base class.

title: Add variant_annotation_sources config to profiles
description: Add variant_annotation_sources dict to app/config/profiles/daly/common.py and app/config/profiles/finngen/common.py with finngen file paths. Re-export from app/config/common.py. Daly: gs://daly-genetics-results/variant_annotations/R13_annotated_variants_v0.small.gz, Finngen: gs://finngen-commons/results_api_data/variant_annotations/R13_annotated_variants_v0.small.gz

title: Feature: Variant Annotation API Endpoint
description: New /api/v1/variant_annotation/{source} endpoint serving FinnGen variant annotation data from tabix-indexed files on GCS. Supports query by variant list, chromosome range, and gene name. GET for single queries, POST for batch. Streams TSV/JSON. Source path param allows future annotation sources beyond finngen. File: R13_annotated_variants_v0.small.gz with columns: variant, chr, pos, ref, alt, INFO, AF, AC_Het, AC_Hom, most_severe, gene_most_severe, rsid, EXOME_enrichment_nfe, GENOME_enrichment_nfe, index.

title: genebass /search results emit wrong data_type (exome for gene_based)
description: Follow-up from genetics-results-api-sc1. get_harmonized_metadata() in app/services/data_access.py dedups iterations by metadata_file (seen_metadata_files). genebass_exome and genebass_gene_based share genebass_pheno_results.txt.bgz, so when include_data_type=True every genebass phenotype is tagged with the first-iterated dataset's data_type ('exome'), including the gene_based ones. Surfaced now that /search emits data_type. Impact: narrow — genebass has no summary stats so has_summary_stats stays correctly False and the consumer never calls summary_stats/genebass/{data_type}; the data_type label itself is just cosmetically wrong. Reviewer recommended deciding (with owner) whether genebass needs correct per-row data_type, vs emitting None/empty when a metadata_file maps to >1 data_type for a resource (safer than confidently-wrong), vs leaving as-is. Files: app/services/data_access.py.
close_reason: Fixed via b-rows: get_harmonized_metadata groups datasets by metadata_file and expands each phenotype per data_type when include_data_type=True. genebass now emits both exome and gene_based (9058=4529+4529, every code in both); /metadata path unchanged (4529, no data_type key); single-dataset resources unaffected. search dedup key includes data_type. Tests/lint/imports pass, review + blind validation approved.
